counters.description: brand pokes you hard and makes it hard to help your team. moving around as much as possible is the only thing you can do in this fight, or wait and try to catch him on cooldown.

galio just hard counters all your tools except your ultimate. he has more disruption to interrupt your engagement, thus making it hard for you to peel for your team. he also has access to poke that you can't match.
throughout the game, you must be mindful of janna's displacement abilities (q, w and r). in this fight, wait for her to misposition. she is immobile; you can root her with your ult if necessary.

it's the one matchup where it's a game of chance since you must always respect morgana's black shield and target multiple champions when you activate solar flare to engage. doing this will make it hard for her to prioritize her black shield.

poppy can prove difficult in lane if she activates steadfast presence to shut down leona's zenith blade engagement (causing you to be stunned on contact). for best results, try to bait out steadfast presence before activating zenith blade, potentially giving you a leg up in the upcoming dive.

senna can be a difficult opponent due to her passive and heavy poke. positioning is critical to isolate her in combat; therefore, this match involves patience and timing to win, but be mindful that she outscales you if left unchecked.

seraphine is a strong poke champion leona wants to avoid being close to when the threat of her ultimate (encore) is looming. to deal with this, you must catch her sleeping and hit her when she least expects it.

due to shen's shadow dash and spirit refuge, he can neutralize your pressure against his team. therefore, you need to engage whenever he has those skills during cooldown. the other troublesome thing about shen is when you dive (with zenith blade) or solar flare a target, he could just activate stand united and make them target harder to kill. exercise caution when dueling shen and his team.

swain is a lane bully support who will make things complicated for you. trying to get farm for your bot lane will take much work since he can use e and pull you towards him and his carry. that can help you in some cases because you stun him back or root him, but you might be in trouble if you are drawn toward him and have no backup. so, play the lane cautiously versus swain.

it's a skill matchup versus taric. he can soak up your damage and heal it out, so you need to attack him when all his skills are on cooldown. also, watch for his ultimate when you decide to all in a target since it makes all his nearby teammates invulnerable. as stated before, it's a coin flip, so play this one safe.

like most heavy damage mages, vel'koz spams out skills quickly and does everything he can to force you back to the fountain. the only way to beat him is to catch him napping and force him back as often as possible.
there's no laning into xerath because he has too much mana sustain and can poke you down pretty hard. instead, punish him if he slips up and falls victim to your crowd control.

synergies.description: with his various weapon stances, he can be difficult to time your crowd control with, but good things can happen if you coordinate well.
ashe has good ways to make your crowd control easier to hit, but it's only sometimes guaranteed to beat them. so make sure you both are on the same page when going for an all-in.
caitlyn and leona are pretty standard, but again, there's not much to scream home about; still, this synergy could be better, but solid coordination can make you both very strong in fights.

the ideal support for draven because of his need to snowball the lane quickly. it's effective because you can root and stun them to make it easier for him to cash in on his adoration stacks and promptly get to his power spikes.
sadly, ezreal doesn't pair well with leona compared to other supports in your pool (lux, soraka, or seraphine). so play the lane like usual and play safe to get clean picks.
this duo is pretty spicy since your stuns can lead to some explosive damage for jhin since you enable his w and make it easy for him to land a dark grenade/lotus trap or 4th shot pretty effectively.
leona and jinx have some synergy if you can trap a target with your roots/stuns and follow that up with jinx's chompers or vice versa. however, it can be challenging for the opponent.
this duo is okay to have in the lane but to make the most of this, you need to time your passive with her passive so you can deal the most damage quickly and efficiently.
since kalista has low mobility, leona can make it so she can get out of dodge with her whole kit. also, if your kalista turns you into the enemy, you can make some delightful plays in team fights.
kog'maw can work with you to align your crowd control, but he doesn't perform best since he prefers buffer supports (lulu, karma or janna). so try to keep him alive as long as possible.
what can i say? lucian loves heavy stun supports. your passive goes nicely with lucian's kit and allows for powerful plays.

miss fortune loves to have a tank with heavy crowd control setting her up, and leona is no exception. she makes all miss fortunes' skills powerful, and your solar flare with her bullet time is just icing on the cake in team fights.
the two of you can make a splash in the lane. once you hit any of your stuns or roots, you allow nilah's q, e and r to do massive damage.

since samira's passive applies to displacement abilities (roots and stuns, to name a few), leona compliments her. whenever you hit shield of daybreak, zenith blade or solar flare, samira's passive triggers instantly can add up when she sets up her combos off yours; they are a solid duo pair in the bot lane.

you don't mesh, but you can still make it work. for example, if you can't get a good e or r or cannot get a clean engage, sivir can pop her r for you and put you within range to make that happen. this duo requires a lot of timing and coordination.

while smolder's "achoo!" and ultimate can help slow down the target to ensure you land your zenith blade/shield of daybreak combo or stun/slow them with a solar flare, i feel he isn't as effective with leona compared to most other pairings. proper ability timings will be crucial to ensure success in this pairing.

a solid choice for leona to have in a lane with because when you clean engage, tristana can follow you up with her w and then pepper them with e, and the two of you can make a quick meal out of your opponent. it's a great duo in bot lane, but it could be better.
i hate to say this, but twitch has yet to mesh with you regarding a kit perspective. if you choose to go with this duo, play the lane safely, and you have no reason to falter.

varus q and r are the reasons you are both a great pair. when your engagement goes, he can follow up with q or even slow them down with his e. you each have good crowd-control options that make the lane strong.

vayne isn't a great pick with you because if you don't coordinate, her knockback (condemn) could mess up your tools to lock someone down and get the kill. so please coordinate with your vayne to ensure she doesn't throw away the kill opportunity.

leona offers xayah a clean way to land all her feathers with q after you have engaged with all your skills. you hit e and r, then follow up with q; xayah can help, then ult off your confrontation, get her feathers to either root them or finish up with a clean kill.
this champion is the same as twitch in terms of having no synergy with you. also, it takes too long to get going. still, please do your best to keep her alive.
with his various weapon stances, he can be difficult to time your crowd control with, but good things can happen if you coordinate well.
ashe has good ways to make your crowd control easier to hit, but it's only sometimes guaranteed to beat them. so make sure you both are on the same page when going for an all-in.
